reasoning:

Under T+1 settlement, the ex-dividend date is also Feb 20. This will cause the stock to adjust downwards by ~$0.68 (or -0.34%) right before the first resolution date of Feb 22 (which uses the Feb 20 close price). Because there are only 4 trading days between Feb 13 and Feb 20 (Feb 16 is a market holiday), the baseline positive drift over 4 days is negligible compared to the $0.68 drop, making a lower close price more likely (~46%). 

For the March 17 resolution date, there are ~21 trading days. Over this period, the expected positive drift (around +0.8% or +$1.60) outweighs the $0.68 dividend drop. Coupled with higher variance over a longer timeframe, this pushes the probability slightly above 50% (~52%). This satisfies both mathematical logic and the instruction to keep distant probabilities closer to 0.5.
